differential_diagnoses: Dabrafenib and trametinib-induced systemic inflammatory response syndrome (SIRS) and hepatitis
Anaplasmosis (given New England residence and clinical presentation)
Biliary obstruction (e.g., choledocholithiasis not visualized on ultrasound)
next_steps: Check serum ferritin, triglycerides, fibrinogen, and soluble IL-2 receptor levels to evaluate for HLH
Obtain MRCP to better visualize the biliary tree and rule out choledocholithiasis
Perform liver biopsy with histological and immunohistochemical evaluation
Initiate corticosteroids pending clinical status and biopsy results
Send peripheral blood PCR for Anaplasma phagocytophilum and other tickborne pathogens

differential_diagnoses: Dabrafenib and trametinib-induced systemic inflammatory response syndrome (SIRS)
Drug-induced liver injury (DILI) with systemic hypersensitivity
Autoimmune hepatitis (secondary to immune-mediated drug toxicity)
next_steps: Order serum ferritin, triglycerides, fibrinogen, and soluble IL-2 receptor levels to evaluate for HLH
Obtain liver biopsy with histological and immunohistochemical evaluation
Perform MRCP to evaluate for occult biliary pathology
Check morning cortisol and ACTH levels
Order autoimmune panel (ANA, ASMA, IgG) to evaluate for immune-mediated hepatitis
Consult Hematology and Oncology for urgent multidisciplinary management
Continue strict avoidance of dabrafenib and trametinib
